steps.title: Use a two-variable coefficient regression
steps.content: The answer choices are all quadratic forms in $a$ and $b$. A quick Desmos method is to test several positive $(a,b)$ pairs and regress the result to $pa^2+qab+rb^2$.

steps.title: Read the coefficients and confirm by formula
steps.content:
The regression gives $p=1$, $q=1$, and $r=\frac{1}{4}$, so the expression is
$$a^2+ab+\frac{b^2}{4}$$
The perfect-square formula confirms it:
$$(a+\frac{b}{2})^2=a^2+2a\left(\frac{b}{2}\right)+\left(\frac{b}{2}\right)^2=a^2+ab+\frac{b^2}{4}$$
Choice D is correct.
